articles.title: I left my Manhattan apartment for Ukraine’s front lines. Now I’m fighting drones
articles.description: Days from the fourth anniversary of the Russian invasion, Viktoriia Honcharuk, a medic, is on a mission to save soldiers in ‘kill zones’ with better technology
articles.content: “Over the last year I went from rescuing 100 wounded soldiers a week to not being able to evacuate at all,” she says. “I’ve gone from staying 800 metres from our frontline positions to being 10-15km away. And when I’m in position and we hear on the r... [8548 chars]

articles.title: India’s Drone Revolution: Why Paras Defence and Zen Technologies are key stocks to watch in 2026
articles.description: India's drone ecosystem is scaling rapidly with 38,000+ registered drones and 40,000 certified pilots as of February 2026. While the sector is expanding beyond defence into agriculture and land mapping (SVAMITVA), Paras Defence and Zen Technologies emerge as the top institutional watch-list stocks due to robust order books and technology leadership. This audit explores their Q3 FY26 performance and why high capital efficiency (ROCE) makes them the preferred "Early Mover" plays.
articles.content: India’s drone sector is seeing steady growth. According to a recent release by the Press Information Bureau, as of February 2026, India has more than 38,000 drones registered in the country. Nearly 40,000 remote pilots have been certified. Over 240 t... [10537 chars]

articles.title: PM Modi, Lula ink rare earths agreement as India moves to diversify critical mineral supply chains beyond China
articles.description: India and Brazil sign a rare earth agreement to diversify mineral supply chains, reduce China dependence, boost trade to $30 billion, and deepen technology, defence, and Global South cooperation
articles.content:
India has taken a big step toward securing its access to critical minerals by signing a rare earth cooperation agreement with Brazil. The move is aimed at reducing dependence on China and strengthening supply chain resilience.
The agreement was one o... [2058 chars]

articles.title: Rwanda IT Minister calls on Dr Jitendra, discusses next-generation sectors
articles.description: Excelsior Correspondent NEW DELHI, Feb 21: Union Minister of State (Independent Charge) for Science & Technology, Earth Sciences, and MoS PMO, Personnel, Public Grievances, Pensions, Atomic Energy and Space, Dr. Jitendra Singh, today said India is ready to deepen its engagement with Rwanda in emerging technologies, innovation ecosystems and next-generation sectors including AI, biotechnology, and nuclear energy, as the two countries explored a structured framework for bilateral cooperation. Dr. Jitendra Singh held a bilateral meeting with Paula Ingabire, Minister of […]
articles.content:
Excelsior Correspondent
NEW DELHI, Feb 21: Union Minister of State (Independent Charge) for Science & Technology, Earth Sciences, and MoS PMO, Personnel, Public Grievances, Pensions, Atomic Energy and Space, Dr. Jitendra Singh, today said India is re... [3294 chars]

articles.title: AI tools can help eliminate subjectivity in medical diagnosis: Dr Jitendra
articles.description: Excelsior Correspondent NEW DELHI, Feb 21 : Union Minister of State (Independent Charge) for Science & Technology, Earth Sciences, PMO, Personnel, Public Grievances, Pensions, Atomic Energy and Space, Dr. Jitendra Singh today said that AI-developed tools are set to eliminate subjectivity in medical diagnosis, ensuring more precise and specific treatment for patients. Delivering the inaugural address at the “Medllumina 2026: International Multi Specialty Medical Conference”, the Minister explained that, for example, while a pathologist examining a cancer patient’s biopsy slide […]
articles.content:
Excelsior Correspondent
NEW DELHI, Feb 21 : Union Minister of State (Independent Charge) for Science & Technology, Earth Sciences, PMO, Personnel, Public Grievances, Pensions, Atomic Energy and Space, Dr. Jitendra Singh today said that AI-developed t... [3365 chars]

articles.title: IIM Jammu conducts entrepreneurship awareness at GCET, GDC Hiranagar
articles.description: Excelsior Correspondent JAMMU, Feb 21: The Indian Institute of Management Jammu conducted its 4th and 5th Entrepreneurship Awareness Programmes (EAPs) at Government College of Engineering and Technology Jammu and Government Degree College Hiranagar under its ongoing five-program outreach initiative supported and funded by DC-MSME, Govt of India. The EAP series was designed to introduce young minds to the fundamentals of entrepreneurship, business creation, and emerging opportunities within the MSME ecosystem. By bringing structured entrepreneurial exposure directly to colleges, the initiative […]
articles.content:
Excelsior Correspondent
JAMMU, Feb 21: The Indian Institute of Management Jammu conducted its 4th and 5th Entrepreneurship Awareness Programmes (EAPs) at Government College of Engineering and Technology Jammu and Government Degree College Hiranagar u... [2169 chars]

articles.title: Microsoft names Indian-origin Asha Sharma as CEO of Microsoft Gaming
articles.description: NEW YORK, Feb 21: Technology giant Microsoft has named Indian-origin Asha Sharma as Executive Vice President and CEO of Microsoft Gaming, joining a long and growing list of Indian-descent leaders at the helm of global companies. In a message announcing the appointment, Microsoft Chairman and CEO Satya Nadella Friday said that Sharma has helped build and scale services that reach billions of people and support thriving consumer and developer ecosystems over the last two years in her role at Microsoft, […]
articles.content:
NEW YORK, Feb 21: Technology giant Microsoft has named Indian-origin Asha Sharma as Executive Vice President and CEO of Microsoft Gaming, joining a long and growing list of Indian-descent leaders at the helm of global companies.
In a message announci... [2751 chars]
